dataset: OpenCAD v0.3.2 (Grand Theft Auto V Roleplay CAD System)

pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_opencad.sh line 235 comment states 'Admin user (ID 2)' and line 238 user_departments INSERT confirms user_id=2. Line 217-218 shows 'Admin User' is created as the first regular user after schema initialization.
Code analysis: setup_opencad.sh line 234 comments explicitly list 'Department IDs: 1=Communications(Dispatch), 2=State, 3=Highway, 4=Sheriff, 5=Police, 6=Fire, 7=EMS, 8=Civilian, 9=Roadside'
Code analysis: setup_opencad.sh line 234 and task.json description both confirm department 6 is named 'Fire'. Standard OpenCAD GTAV game data naming convention.

Standard APCO 10-code system verified via web search: 10-8 means 'In Service' or 'Available' in emergency services radio codes. Confirmed in RadioReference.com (https://www.catencode.com/codes/10-8) and APCO standards. Also referenced in task.json description 'mark yourself as Available (10-8)'.

privileged_info_summary: The Admin User account (user_id=2) must be assigned to the Fire Department (department_id=6) and set to status 10-8 (In Service/Available). These are the exact IDs and codes used in the OpenCAD v0.3.2 system deployed in this environment. The Fire Department is one of nine predefined departments in OpenCAD's GTAV game data initialization. The 10-8 radio code is the standard APCO emergency services code meaning the unit is available for dispatch.
